message:
fix(security): constant-time comparisons and length-prefixed hash inputs across crypto subsystems

epoch_scoped_keys.rs:
- DerivedKey::PartialEq now uses constant-time ct_eq_bytes() instead
  of derived == to prevent timing side-channels on key material
- derive_epoch_key() HKDF info switches from formatted string to
  length-prefixed binary encoding ("franken-node:epoch-kdf-info:v1:" +
  epoch_le_bytes + domain_len_le_bytes + domain_bytes) to prevent
  domain/epoch ambiguity in the key derivation
- sign_epoch_artifact() and verify_epoch_signature() both include
  artifact length as u64 LE bytes before payload in HMAC to prevent
  length-extension confusion

threshold_sig.rs:
- Replace plain == for signer_id/key_id strings with constant-time
  ct_eq() to prevent timing side-channels during threshold verification

decision_receipt.rs:
- sha256_hex() includes (bytes.len() as u64).to_le_bytes() before
  payload, preventing concatenation-ambiguity in receipt hashing

state_model.rs:
- compute_hash() includes (canonical.len() as u64).to_le_bytes()
  before canonical JSON, same length-prefix pattern for collision safety

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
